scripture.name: Bhagavad Gita
scripture.description: Dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna in the Mahabharata, revealing dharma, yoga, and liberation.

chapters.theme: The sorrow and moral confusion of Arjuna before battle.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच | धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः | मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ||
verses.transliteration: dhritarashtra uvaca dharmakshetre kurukshetre samaveta yuyutsavah mamakah pandavas caiva kim akurvata sanjaya

translations.simple_meaning: Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya what his sons and the sons of Pandu did after gathering at Kurukshetra, eager for battle.
translations.philosophical_note: The opening question frames the conflict between attachment and righteousness.

translations.simple_meaning: धृतराष्ट्र संजय से पूछते हैं कि युद्ध के इच्छुक मेरे और पाण्डु के पुत्रों ने धर्मभूमि कुरुक्षेत्र में क्या किया।
translations.philosophical_note: आरंभ का प्रश्न आसक्ति और धर्म के टकराव को प्रकट करता है।

chapters.theme: The immortality of the Self and duty without attachment.

verses.sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन् नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः | अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ||
verses.transliteration: na jayate mriyate va kadacin nayam bhutva bhavita va na bhuyah ajo nityah sasvatoyam purano na hanyate hanyamane sarire

translations.simple_meaning: The Self is never born and never dies. It is eternal, ancient, and not destroyed when the body is destroyed.
translations.philosophical_note: Krishna distinguishes the immortal atman from the perishable body to free Arjuna from fear.

translations.simple_meaning: आत्मा न जन्म लेती है, न मरती है। शरीर के नष्ट होने पर भी वह नष्ट नहीं होती।
translations.philosophical_note: श्रीकृष्ण आत्मा और शरीर का भेद बताकर भय को दूर करते हैं।

verses.sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन | मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ||
verses.transliteration: karmany evadhikaras te ma phalesu kadacana ma karma-phala-hetur bhur ma te sango stv akarmani

translations.simple_meaning: You have a right only to action, never to its results. Do not act for reward, and do not cling to inaction.
translations.philosophical_note: This is the foundation of karma yoga: disciplined action offered without egoistic claim.

translations.simple_meaning: तुम्हारा अधिकार केवल कर्म पर है, फल पर कभी नहीं। फल की आसक्ति से कर्म मत करो और अकर्मण्यता में भी मत फंसो।
translations.philosophical_note: यह कर्मयोग का मूल है: निष्काम होकर कर्तव्य का पालन।

chapters.theme: Selfless action as a path to purity and liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर | असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पुरुषः ||
verses.transliteration: tasmad asaktah satatam karyam karma samacara asakto hy acaran karma param apnoti purusah

translations.simple_meaning: Therefore, perform your duty constantly without attachment; by detached action a person reaches the Supreme.
translations.philosophical_note: Detachment purifies intention and turns worldly work into spiritual practice.

translations.simple_meaning: इसलिए आसक्ति रहित होकर अपना कर्तव्य निरंतर करो; ऐसा कर्म मनुष्य को परम अवस्था तक पहुंचाता है।
translations.philosophical_note: अनासक्ति कर्म को साधना बना देती है।

chapters.theme: Wisdom, divine descent, and sanctified action.
Renunciation and action harmonized in yoga.
Discipline of meditation and mastery of mind.
Knowing Krishna as the source of all.
The Imperishable and remembrance at death.
The sovereign knowledge and deepest secret.
The vision of the cosmic form.
Field and knower of the field.
The Supreme Person beyond the world-tree.
Divine and demonic tendencies in human nature.
Threefold faith shaped by the gunas.
Final synthesis of duty, devotion, and liberation.
